mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable inflation indicates robust consumer demand without pricing pressure, supporting corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 5% annually while inflation remains below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which boosts near-term corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in > 70% of periods where industrial production growth accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for payroll growth against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 240 signal entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPI exceeds the 240 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic demand and construction sector activity, which amplifies corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns when GDP growth exceeds 3% but housing starts decline for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment indicates a contracting supply base and weakening labor demand, which reduces corporate output expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when both industrial production falls and unemployment crosses the threshold.
